description:
You are a localization engineer who has been asked to update a very small SQLite
  database that stores user-facing strings.

  Current project layout (already present on disk):
    /home/user/projects/i18n/
        ├── messages.db         ← SQLite database you must modify
        └── (nothing else yet)  ← you will create a log file here

  Your objectives
  1. Open /home/user/projects/i18n/messages.db with the SQLite CLI and inspect the
     table named “translations”.
  2. Insert Spanish (es) translations for the keys “hello” and “goodbye”.
  3. Correct the existing English “goodbye” translation so that the value becomes
     “Good bye” (note the space).
  4. Create a plain-text change log called
     /home/user/projects/i18n/translation_update.log whose exact contents match
     the specification below.

  Required final table contents
    id | language | key      | value
    -----------------------------------------
     1 | en       | hello    | Hello
     2 | en       | goodbye  | Good bye
     3 | es       | hello    | Hola
     4 | es       | goodbye  | Adiós

  Log-file format (MUST match character-for-character):
    Line-1:  #translations_snapshot        (literally the hash sign followed by the word)
    Line-2:  Total rows: 4
    Line-3:  id,language,key,value
    Line-4:  1,en,hello,Hello
    Line-5:  2,en,goodbye,Good bye
    Line-6:  3,es,hello,Hola
    Line-7:  4,es,goodbye,Adiós
    Line-8:  (must be a terminating newline after the last line)

  Validation notes
  • The automated test will:
      – Open /home/user/projects/i18n/messages.db with sqlite3 and run
        “SELECT id,language,key,value FROM translations ORDER BY id;”.
      – Compare the output against the four rows listed above.
      – Read /home/user/projects/i18n/translation_update.log and compare it
        byte-for-byte with the 8-line template (including the final newline).
  • You DO NOT need root privileges.
  • Only standard SQLite CLI commands are allowed; do not use any external SQL client.
  • Do not remove or rename the database file.
  • Make sure all non-ASCII characters (e.g., “Adiós”) are stored correctly in UTF-8.

  Deliverables to create/modify
  1. /home/user/projects/i18n/messages.db   (updated records)
  2. /home/user/projects/i18n/translation_update.log   (exact contents as above)

  Hints
  • Use the auto-incrementing primary key; do not hard-code ids.
  • The entire operation requires no more than 3–4 sqlite3 commands
    (an UPDATE and two INSERTs plus whatever you use to verify).
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
    1. The file /home/user/projects/i18n/messages.db exists.
       Schema:
         CREATE TABLE translations (
             id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
             language TEXT NOT NULL,
             key TEXT NOT NULL,
             value TEXT NOT NULL
         );
       Rows:
         id=1 | language='en' | key='hello'   | value='Hello'
         id=2 | language='en' | key='goodbye' | value='Goodbye'    -- note missing space
    2. No translation_update.log file is present.

  Expected final state after successful completion:
    1. /home/user/projects/i18n/messages.db contains exactly four rows:
         1 | en | hello    | Hello
         2 | en | goodbye  | Good bye
         3 | es | hello    | Hola
         4 | es | goodbye  | Adiós
       (IDs 3 and 4 may be higher if the agent mistakenly
        inserts & deletes rows, but the automated grader will allow only
        the four rows with those exact values and IDs <= 4.)
    2. /home/user/projects/i18n/translation_update.log exists and contains:

       #translations_snapshot
       Total rows: 4
       id,language,key,value
       1,en,hello,Hello
       2,en,goodbye,Good bye
       3,es,hello,Hola
       4,es,goodbye,Adiós
       ⏎  (terminal newline)

       The grader will read the file and compare it byte-for-byte, including the
       final newline character (ASCII 0x0A).
